headline: Federal Council informed of NCS implementation report (May 20 meeting).
summary: The Federal Council at its regular May 20 meeting received the latest progress report on implementation of the National Cyberstrategy (NCS), documenting 2025 activity across all 17 NCS III measures.

headline: NCSC Annual Report 2025: 64,733 incident reports; mandatory critical-infrastructure reporting live.
summary: Switzerland's NCSC published its 2025 annual report (released early 2026), logging 64,733 voluntary cyber incident reports — roughly 2,000 more than 2024.

headline: National Council rejects Neutrality Initiative (128–60); SVP stands alone.
summary: After roughly eight hours of chamber debate, the National Council voted 128 to 60 against recommending the right-wing people's initiative that would constitutionally entrench Switzerland's "absolute" neutrality.

headline: Bilaterals III dispatch to Parliament: summer-session debate underway.
summary: The Federal Council approved its dispatch to Parliament on the Bilaterals III (Switzerland–EU stabilisation) package on 13 March 2026; both chambers began formal debate during the spring session.

headline: Vaud: CHF 75 million refugee-cost exposure as federal support winds down.
summary: Vaud estimates it faces CHF 25 million per year in additional Ukrainian-refugee social-welfare expenditure — rising to CHF 75 million if the federal government's savings-driven withdrawal of supplementary funding goes ahead as planned.

headline: Trump–Xi Beijing summit (14–15 May): Taiwan dominates, no concrete deal.
summary: The first US state visit to China since 2017 produced sharp public language but no announced agreements.

headline: Iran–US Hormuz standoff: ceasefire holds, Strait still closed; Barakah nuclear plant hit by drone.
summary: The April 8 ceasefire between the US/Israel coalition and Iran remains nominally in effect, but the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed — Iran declared it shut on 4 March and shipping has not meaningfully resumed.

headline: Russia–Ukraine: largest aerial campaign of the war followed by mass retaliatory strikes.
summary: Russia launched its largest two-day aerial offensive of the conflict on 13–14 May, firing over 1,560 drones and ballistic missiles across Ukraine; a direct hit on a Kyiv apartment building killed nine people and left dozens injured.

headline: EU forging "hedging alliance" with Indo-Pacific middle powers; Trump floats G7 restructure.
summary: French President Macron's April tour of Tokyo and Seoul produced unusually direct language warning medium-sized nations against becoming "vassals of two hegemonic powers" (the US and China); he called for a "coalition of independents." The EU signed its first two Security and Defence Partnerships (S

headline: India–Pakistan: one year since Operation Sindoor ceasefire, holding uneasily.
summary: As of early May 2026, India and Pakistan are a year past the May 10, 2025 ceasefire that ended four days of the most intense military conflict between the nuclear-armed neighbours since 1971 — Indian strikes reached the outskirts of Islamabad and hit Lahore's air base.

headline: CVE-2026-42897 — Microsoft Exchange Server OWA XSS zero-day (CVSS 8.1 / NVD 6.1, actively exploited, no patch).
summary: An improper-neutralisation (CWE-79) flaw in Outlook Web Access allows a remote attacker to execute arbitrary JavaScript in a victim's browser by sending a specially crafted email that the target merely needs to open in OWA.

headline: Drupal PSA-2026-05-18 — "Highly critical" unauthenticated core flaw patched today (17:00–21:00 UTC).
summary: The Drupal Security Team rated the vulnerability 20 out of 25 on its own scale, with Access Complexity: None and Authentication: None — meaning exploitation requires no privileges and trivial effort.

headline: Grafana Labs GitHub breach via TanStack npm supply-chain attack; source code exfiltrated.
summary: Grafana Labs confirmed on 19 May that its entire GitHub codebase — including private repositories with internal operational details — was downloaded by attackers following a supply-chain compromise through the TanStack npm package (orchestrated by the threat actor group TeamPCP, the same campaign th

headline: CVE-2026-32202 — Windows Shell zero-click NTLM coercion (APT28, actively exploited).
summary: A protection-mechanism failure in Windows Shell stemming from an incomplete fix for CVE-2026-21510: when a user opens any folder containing a malicious `.LNK` file — no double-click required — Windows automatically initiates an SMB connection to an attacker-controlled server, sending the victim's Ne

headline: CVE-2026-20182 — Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN auth bypass (CVSS 10.0, KEV, UAT-8616).
summary: An authentication bypass in the SD-WAN peering mechanism (vdaemon, DTLS UDP port 12346) allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to claim vHub device identity, bypassing certificate verification and obtaining full admin access to the SD-WAN Controller (formerly vSmart) and Manager (formerly vManage

headline: CVE-2026-31431 — Linux kernel "Copy Fail" local privilege escalation (CVSS 7.8, KEV since 1 May).
summary: A high-severity LPE flaw affecting all major Linux distributions allows an unprivileged local user to obtain root via a race condition in the kernel's copy-on-write path; impact is amplified in cloud and Kubernetes environments where multi-tenant workloads share a kernel.
